name: invest-09-security-audit-triage
initialPrompt: Our annual third-party security audit came back with 47 findings ranging from critical to informational. We need to triage and remediate these systematically without stopping feature work entirely. I need a plan to: classify findings by exploitability and business impact, fix all critical and high findings within 30 days, establish a process for medium and low findings, and set up automated scanning so we catch new vulnerabilities before the next audit.

cannedAuthorResponse.title: Security audit triage and systematic remediation

blocks.text: Remediate all critical/high audit findings within 30 days and establish continuous vulnerability scanning
successCriteria: zero open critical findings after 30 days
all high findings fixed or formally risk-accepted
automated scanner runs on every PR

blocks.title: Build triage matrix: score each finding by CVSS, exploitability, and data exposure
Fix all P0 critical findings with immediate hotfixes
acceptance: each critical finding has a merged fix
auditor confirmation of closure for each

blocks.question: For high findings that cannot be fixed within 30 days, should we formally risk-accept or request extension?

options.label: Formal risk acceptance with CISO sign-off

blocks.title: Set up automated SAST/DAST scanner (e.g. Semgrep + OWASP ZAP) in CI pipeline
acceptance: findings above severity threshold block merge

blocks.description: Fixed vulnerabilities re-introduced by future feature work without awareness
blocks.mitigation: Add security regression tests for each fixed critical; scanner blocks reintroduction

blocks.question: Should we launch a bug bounty program to get continuous external testing between formal audits?

cannedForcedReviseResponse.title: Security audit triage and systematic remediation

blocks.text: Remediate all critical/high audit findings within 30 days and establish continuous vulnerability scanning
successCriteria: zero open critical findings after 30 days
all high findings fixed or formally risk-accepted
automated scanner runs on every PR
remediation status reported weekly to CISO

blocks.title: Hold kickoff with auditor to clarify ambiguous findings and confirm reproduction steps
acceptance: all 47 findings have clear reproduction steps

blocks.title: Build triage matrix: score each finding by CVSS, exploitability, and data exposure
Fix all P0 critical findings with immediate hotfixes
acceptance: each critical finding has a merged fix
auditor confirmation of closure for each

blocks.question: For high findings that cannot be fixed within 30 days, should we formally risk-accept or request extension?

options.label: Formal risk acceptance with CISO sign-off

blocks.chosen: Formal risk acceptance with CISO sign-off
blocks.rationale: Extension delays close-out; risk acceptance is the standard process and keeps the timeline.

blocks.title: Set up automated SAST/DAST scanner (e.g. Semgrep + OWASP ZAP) in CI pipeline
acceptance: findings above severity threshold block merge

blocks.title: Publish weekly remediation status report for CISO and engineering leadership
acceptance: first report sent within 3 days of triage completion

blocks.description: Fixed vulnerabilities re-introduced by future feature work without awareness
blocks.mitigation: Add security regression tests for each fixed critical; scanner blocks reintroduction

blocks.question: Should we launch a bug bounty program to get continuous external testing between formal audits?

idChangedButCorresponding.note: Same intent (bug bounty program consideration) but id renamed during revise. AC-13 re-anchoring maps a comment on oq-bug-bounty to oq-bounty-program.

decoy.note: Genuinely new block. Shares surface tokens 'remediation', 'triage', and 'CISO' with task-triage-matrix. A comment on task-triage-matrix must stay there, not mis-attach to task-remediation-report.

notes: Renumbering pressure: task-auditor-kickoff inserted near top; task-remediation-report added as genuinely new; oq-bug-bounty renamed. Positional shift after insertion breaks naive mapping for task-triage-matrix onward. expectedPreservedIds excludes renamed OQ and both new tasks.
